message.content: Adversaries may exploit open technical databases related to Content Delivery Networks (CDNs) to gather information about a target organization. CDNs distribute content across multiple servers to enhance performance and availability, often customizing delivery based on the requestor’s location. Threat actors can use online tools to identify and analyze CDN data, potentially uncovering sensitive information due to misconfigurations or insufficient protections. This information can facilitate further reconnaissance, such as active scanning or searching open websites/domains, and may aid in establishing operational resources or gaining initial access through methods like drive-by compromise. Mitigation strategies include regularly auditing CDN configurations, ensuring robust access controls, and monitoring for unauthorized access or anomalies in CDN traffic.
